system_prompt: You are participating in a research study about how AI models use and attribute information sources in AI-for-software-engineering questions. Answer carefully. Do not invent papers, URLs, DOIs, or venue facts. If uncertain, say so.
user_prompt:
Rank 15 venues for AI-assisted software engineering, but include both top venues and lower-rank or more specialized venues. Consider ICSE, FSE, ASE, ISSTA, MSR, ICSME, SANER, RE, MODELS, ICPC, COMPSAC, ICST, SAC, PLDI, CAV, ICLR, NeurIPS, CHI, and UIST. Explain why lower-rank or specialized venues may still matter for source retrieval.

Return JSON with:
- ranked_items: list of objects with rank, acronym, full_name, justification
- references: list of objects with title, url, doi, arxiv_id, source_type, claimed_purpose
- uncertainty_notes
response_text:
The user wants me to rank 15 venues for AI-assisted software engineering from a given list of 19 venues. They want both top venues and lower-rank/specialized venues. I need to provide a JSON with ranked_items (15 items), references, and uncertainty_notes.

Let me think about the venues provided:
1. ICSE - International Conference on Software Engineering (top tier)
2. FSE - Foundations of Software Engineering (top tier, formerly SIGSOFT FSE)
3. ASE - Automated Software Engineering (top tier)
4. ISSTA - International Symposium on Software Testing and Analysis (top tier)
5. MSR - Mining Software Repositories (specialized, highly relevant)
6. ICSME - International Conference on Software Maintenance and Evolution (specialized)
7. SANER - International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering (specialized)
8. RE - Requirements Engineering Conference (specialized)
9. MODELS - ACM/IEEE International Conference on Model Driven Engineering Languages and Systems (specialized)
10. ICPC - International Conference on Program Comprehension (specialized)
11. COMPSAC - IEEE International Conference on Computer Software and Applications (broader, lower tier)
12. ICST - IEEE International Conference on Software Testing, Verification and Validation (specialized)
13. SAC - ACM Symposium on Applied Computing (broad, lower tier)
14. PLDI - Programming Language Design and Implementation (top tier PL, relevant for AI/SE intersection)
15. CAV - Computer Aided Verification (top tier verification, relevant)
16. ICLR - International Conference on Learning Representations (top tier ML, relevant for AI methods)
17. NeurIPS - Neural Information Processing Systems (top tier ML, relevant for AI methods)
18. CHI - Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (top tier HCI, relevant for human-AI interaction in SE)
19. UIST - User Interface Software and Technology (top tier HCI/UI, relevant)

I need to rank 15 of these. The user said "include both top venues and lower-rank or more specialized venues" from this list. So I should pick 15 that represent a good mix.

For AI-assisted software engineering specifically, the most relevant would be:
- Core SE venues: ICSE, FSE, ASE, ISSTA
- Specialized SE venues highly relevant:
